publications.title: Structure of a fully assembled tumor-specific T cell receptor ligated by pMHC.
publications.authors: Sušac L, Vuong MT, Thomas C, von Bülow S, O'Brien-Ball C, Santos AM, Fernandes RA, Hummer G, Tampé R, Davis SJ.
publications.executive_summary: This study determined the cryo-EM structure of a tumor-reactive human αβTCR (origin and specificity not specified beyond "melanoma-specific") bound to a class I pMHC, capturing the complete 11-subunit TCR/CD3 signaling complex at 3.08 Å resolution. The structure reveals how multivalent interactions across three layers stabilize the complex, including a role for membrane-embedded sterol lipids in TCR assembly and clustered cysteines stabilizing the CD3 heterodimers. The work establishes the spatial geometry required for T cells to scan antigen-presenting cell membranes efficiently and demonstrates that TCR triggering can occur without large conformational rearrangements in the ligand-bound state. These insights into how tumor-specific TCRs recognize and bind pMHC are relevant to understanding T cell activation against cancer and may inform rational engineering of TCRs for immunotherapy applications.
